brand.name: Backup Power Pro
brand.tagline: One-Day Generator Inlet Installation. Done Right.
brand.service: Generator inlet and interlock kit installation for portable generators
brand.location: Upstate South Carolina — Greenville, Pickens, Spartanburg counties
brand.cta_subtext: Takes 30 seconds. We'll text you your all-in number today.
brand.cta_note: The customer fills out a 30-second form. We TEXT THEM back with their all-in price the same day. We do NOT call. There is NO appointment, NO scheduling, NO salesperson visit. The form button on the site says 'Text Me My Price'. Use 'Get Your Exact Price' as the primary CTA in ads — it matches the landing page exactly.

colors._note: These are the EXACT brand colors from the website CSS. NEVER use shiny/metallic gold gradients. The gold is a flat bright yellow.

gradients._note: Navy-to-blue gradient, NOT metallic gold. Gold is always flat/solid.

typography.style: Clean, modern, professional. Short punchy sentences. Not salesy — expert authority.

what_we_actually_install._note: Use ONLY these products and descriptions. Never invent fake equipment.

inlet_box.appearance: Gray metal box with round twist-lock cover, mounted on exterior wall
inlet_box.mounting: Exterior wall near breaker panel — on siding, brick, or block
inlet_box.conduit: Gray electrical conduit running from inlet to panel

interlock_kit.description: Mechanical interlock on breaker panel that prevents backfeed to utility
interlock_kit.appearance: Metal sliding plate on panel face, painted or bare metal
interlock_kit.function: Forces main breaker off before generator breaker can turn on — prevents electrocuting lineworkers

generator_cord.appearance: Yellow, thick, with twist-lock connectors on both ends

surge_protector.appearance: Small gray box mounted near panel
surge_protector.note: Optional add-on, not included in base install

customers_generator._note: We do NOT sell generators. The customer already owns one. Most common brands we see:

our_demo_unit.appearance: Red and black portable generator with pull-start

accurate_messaging._critical: A portable generator CANNOT power the entire house simultaneously. It powers SELECTED circuits through the breaker panel via the interlock. The homeowner chooses which breakers to turn on based on their generator's capacity. Do NOT say 'powers your whole house' — say 'power your essentials through the breaker panel'.

_ad_rules_MUST_READ_BEFORE_EVERY_GENERAT.NEVER_mention_HVAC_or_AC: Do NOT say 'HVAC', 'air conditioning', 'AC', 'heat pump', or 'central air' in any ad. A typical portable generator cannot run HVAC. Only list the approved essentials above.
_ad_rules_MUST_READ_BEFORE_EVERY_GENERAT.NEVER_say_80_percent_standby: Do NOT say '80% of a standby generator' or any percentage comparison to standby. This is not factual. Remove from all ads.
_ad_rules_MUST_READ_BEFORE_EVERY_GENERAT.no_scheduling_no_appointments: We do NOT schedule appointments. Do NOT say 'schedule', 'book an appointment', 'book now', 'schedule below', 'schedule a call', or 'book your assessment'. The lead form sends a text — we communicate entirely via TEXT MESSAGE.
_ad_rules_MUST_READ_BEFORE_EVERY_GENERAT.no_calling: Do NOT say 'call us', 'give us a call', 'one phone call', 'one call', or any call-to-action involving calling. We only communicate via text.
correct_cta_language: Get Your Exact Price — Takes 30 Seconds ↓
Fill out the form below. We'll text you today. ↓
_ad_rules_MUST_READ_BEFORE_EVERY_GENERAT.NEVER_say_text_us: Do NOT say 'text us' — WE text THEM. The customer fills out a form; we text them back. Say 'we'll text you' not 'text us'.
_ad_rules_MUST_READ_BEFORE_EVERY_GENERAT.how_it_works: Customer fills out a 30-second form on the website. We text THEM back with their all-in price the same day. No calls, no appointments, no salesperson visit. The CTA on the site is 'Get Your Exact Price' and the form button says 'Text Me My Price'.

what_it_does: Powers your essentials through your breaker panel — no extension cords
You choose which circuits to run based on your generator's wattage
Typical essentials: fridge, freezer, sump pump, well pump, garage door, water heater, WiFi, lighting, outlets, microwave
One cord plugs into the outside of your house
Flip the interlock, turn on the breakers you need
approved_claims: Free quote — we text you back
never_say: HVAC or AC or air conditioning (typical portable gens can't run it)
80% of a standby generator (not factual, made up)
Backup generator installation (we don't install generators)
Schedule / book an appointment / book now (we don't schedule — text only)
Call us / give us a call / one phone call (we only text)

wrong_way_problems: Carbon monoxide risk — generator near open windows
Fire hazard — overloaded cords, daisy-chained power strips
Only powers a few items — fridge and a couple lamps
Open windows in a storm — rain, cold, insects
right_way_benefits: Power your essentials through your breaker panel
No extension cords, no open windows
One cord plugs into outside of house

comparison_vs_standby.standby_note: A fraction of the cost of a standby system — powers everything that matters

ad_design_rules.style: Clean, modern, professional. NOT flashy, NOT salesy, NOT 'as seen on TV'. Think Apple-level simplicity with trade-professional credibility.
ad_design_rules.backgrounds: White (#ffffff), light blue (#e8f1f8), or navy gradient. Never busy patterns.
ad_design_rules.accent_usage: Gold (#ffba00) for buttons, highlights, checkmarks, accent lines. NEVER as a large background. NEVER as a shiny/metallic gradient.
ad_design_rules.danger_color: #dc2626 red for 'wrong way' content, extension cord warnings
ad_design_rules.success_color: #16a34a green for 'right way' checkmarks (on light backgrounds)
ad_design_rules.safe_zones: 14% margin on all sides for Meta feed ads
ad_design_rules.cta_rule: NEVER include a website URL or link in ad images. Meta ads have a button below the image that links to the site. Use directional CTAs instead like 'Get Your Exact Price ↓' or 'See Your Price in 30 Seconds ↓' or 'Learn More ↓'. BPP is TEXT-ONLY, so NEVER use 'Book', 'Schedule', 'Call', 'Video Assessment', or any appointment language (see accurate_messaging).

photo_treatment.wrong_way: Dark, moody, slightly desaturated. Slight red/warm tint overlay. Should feel unsafe/messy.
photo_treatment.right_way: Bright, clean, natural daylight. Slight cool/blue tint. Should feel professional/trustworthy.

graphic_design_language._note: Use these terms in Nano Banana prompts to produce on-brand results. This is the visual vocabulary for BPP.
graphic_design_language.overall_aesthetic: Minimal corporate utility. Clean lines, generous whitespace, confident restraint. The design should feel like a licensed professional's marketing — not a DIY Canva template and not a flashy infomercial. Think: trusted contractor, modern website, Apple-level simplicity applied to a blue-collar trade.

layout_principles.grid: Strong vertical and horizontal alignment. Content snaps to an invisible grid. Nothing floats loosely.
layout_principles.whitespace: Generous negative space. Let elements breathe. Don't fill every pixel — emptiness signals confidence.
layout_principles.hierarchy: One dominant element per frame (headline OR photo, not both competing). Clear reading order: headline → image → supporting text → CTA.
layout_principles.symmetry: Prefer balanced compositions. For comparison ads: equal weight on both sides with a clean center divider.
layout_principles.grouping: Related items clustered tightly, separated from other groups by whitespace. Proximity = relationship.

headings.case: Sentence case or uppercase for short labels (3 words max). NEVER all-caps for full sentences.
headings.tracking: Tight letter-spacing (-1px to -2px) for large display text. Normal for body.

labels.color: #ffba00 gold on dark backgrounds, #0b1f3b navy on light

numbers_prices.style: Tight tracking, large size for emphasis. Gold (#ffba00) for value/price callouts.

color_application.60_30_10_rule: 60% neutral (white or navy), 30% secondary (light blue or navy-mid), 10% accent (gold). Never more than 10% gold.
color_application.navy_usage: Primary backgrounds, headings, authority elements. Use the gradient (navy → navy-mid → navy-light) for large areas.
color_application.gold_usage: Buttons, checkmarks, accent lines, price callouts, small highlights. Always flat — no drop shadows, no metallic sheen, no 3D effects.
color_application.red_usage: ONLY for danger/warning content. Extension cord 'wrong way' sections. Red (#dc2626) text or red-tinted overlays on 'bad' photos.
color_application.green_usage: ONLY for success/checkmark elements on light backgrounds. On dark (navy) backgrounds, use gold checkmarks instead.
color_application.white_usage: Primary background for clean, open layouts. Text on navy or red backgrounds.

visual_elements.dividers: Thin (1-3px) lines in light gray (#ddd) or subtle navy. Used to separate sections, especially in comparison layouts.
visual_elements.cards: Rounded corners (16-20px). Very subtle border (1px, rgba(11,31,59,0.07)). White background. Optional hover shadow.
visual_elements.buttons: Pill-shaped (border-radius: 100px). Gold background with navy text, OR navy background with white text. Font: Outfit 600.
visual_elements.icons: Simple, line-style or flat. Navy colored. No 3D, no gradients in icons.
visual_elements.checkmarks: Gold (#ffba00) on dark backgrounds. Green (#16a34a) on light backgrounds.
visual_elements.x_marks: Red (#dc2626) or light red (#ffcccc) on red backgrounds.
visual_elements.accent_lines: Thin gold (#ffba00) lines as subtle section separators or top-borders on cards. Can use gold gradient: linear-gradient(90deg, transparent, #ffba00, transparent).
visual_elements.overlays: Semi-transparent color overlays on photos. Red tint (rgba(220,38,38,0.15)) for danger. Navy tint (rgba(11,31,59,0.1)) for professional.

CRITICAL_PHOTO_REALISM_RULES._note: These rules must be applied to EVERY photo-based generation. Violations make the image look AI-generated and destroy trust with the target audience.
CRITICAL_PHOTO_REALISM_RULES.camera_style: Photorealistic. Looks like it was taken with a modern iPhone (iPhone 15 Pro level quality) by someone who knows what they're doing — but NOT staged or posed. Candid, natural, documentary feel. Not studio lighting, not stock photo composition, not overly polished.
CRITICAL_PHOTO_REALISM_RULES.NOT_staged: Photos should feel like they were taken on the job site, mid-work or just after completion. The scene is real, not set up for a photo shoot. Slightly imperfect framing is fine — that's what makes it believable.
CRITICAL_PHOTO_REALISM_RULES.neighborhood_aesthetic: Upper-middle-class and upscale neighborhoods only. Well-maintained homes. These details must appear: nicely landscaped yards, mature trees, clean driveways, quality construction (brick, Hardie board siding, stone accents, wood trim). Think: Greenville SC neighborhoods like Chanticleer, Augusta Road corridor, Verdae, Five Forks area. $400k-$900k homes. NOT apartments, NOT run-down properties, NOT plain builder-grade vinyl.
CRITICAL_PHOTO_REALISM_RULES.brick_alignment_CRITICAL: BRICK AND SIDING MUST BE PERFECTLY ALIGNED. This is one of the most common AI failure points. Every course of brick must be level, horizontal, and consistent in height. Mortar joints must be uniform. Siding panels (lap siding, Hardie board) must be perfectly parallel, evenly spaced, with no warping or misalignment. Before finalizing any photo generation prompt, explicitly instruct: 'All brick courses are perfectly level and aligned, mortar joints are uniform, siding panels are perfectly parallel and evenly spaced with no distortion or misalignment.' Crooked bricks or wavy siding immediately signal AI generation to the viewer.
CRITICAL_PHOTO_REALISM_RULES.siding_alignment_CRITICAL: Vinyl or fiber cement lap siding must have perfectly horizontal parallel lines. Each board overlaps the one below it uniformly. No waves, no bowing, no perspective distortion on straight surfaces. The lines should be laser-straight.
prompt_phrases_to_always_include: perfectly level brick courses with uniform mortar joints
shot on iPhone 15 Pro, natural light, no flash

photo_direction.general_style: Photorealistic iPhone-quality photography. Natural, candid, unstaged. Not stock photo polished. Real job site feel shot by a professional with a phone.
photo_direction.lighting_right_way: Natural daylight, bright, high contrast, clean shadows. The kind of light that makes a job site look professional.
photo_direction.lighting_wrong_way: Dark, underexposed, single harsh light source (work lamp or flashlight). Moody shadows. The kind of light that makes everything look sketchy.
photo_direction.composition: Rule of thirds. Subject fills the frame. Shallow depth of field for detail shots. Wide angle for full setup shots.
photo_direction.color_grade_right: Slightly cool/desaturated. Clean whites. Think: contractor's portfolio photo.
photo_direction.color_grade_wrong: Warm/amber cast from work lamp. Deep shadows. Slightly muddy. Think: news report about a house fire.
what_to_show_right: Reliance Controls inlet box (gray, round cover) mounted cleanly on exterior wall
Generator on concrete/driveway with single yellow cord to inlet
what_to_show_wrong: Orange extension cords snaking through cracked windows
Cords draped across floors, doorways, furniture
Generator running dangerously close to open window
Dark interior lit only by a work lamp
never_show: Generator running indoors (this is lethal, not just 'wrong')
Before/after that implies our work replaced someone else's

physical_reality_rules._note: AI image generators ignore physics. Every prompt MUST specify how things physically connect and work to avoid impossible scenes.
extension_cord_wrong_way: Cord starts at generator OUTSIDE, runs THROUGH a cracked/open window INTO the house
Inside the house, the cord plugs into a POWER STRIP or MULTI-OUTLET, not into a wall outlet
Appliances (lamp, fridge, fan) plug into THAT power strip — they do NOT plug into the wall
The wall outlets are DEAD during an outage — nothing should be plugged into them
Multiple cords may run through different windows to reach different rooms
inlet_right_way: Generator sits OUTSIDE on concrete/driveway, never indoors
Single yellow L14-30 cord runs from generator to the INLET BOX on the exterior wall
Inlet box is a gray Reliance Controls box mounted on siding/brick/block
Gray conduit runs from inlet box THROUGH the wall to the breaker panel INSIDE
Interlock kit is on the breaker panel face — a metal sliding plate
Power flows: generator → cord → inlet → conduit → panel → house circuits

connector_gender_CRITICAL._note: This is a common AI generation error that makes images look wrong to anyone who knows generators. Get this right every time.
connector_gender_CRITICAL.inlet_box: The inlet box on the wall has MALE prongs — the pins stick OUT from the face of the inlet. It is a male connector.
connector_gender_CRITICAL.cord_wall_end: The cord end that plugs INTO the inlet box has a FEMALE receptacle — it has holes/slots that receive the male prongs of the inlet. The cord plugs ONTO the inlet.
connector_gender_CRITICAL.cord_generator_end: The other end of the cord (at the generator) also has a female receptacle that plugs into the generator's outlet — same principle.
connector_gender_CRITICAL.prompt_instruction: Always specify in prompts: 'The inlet box on the wall has male prongs protruding outward. The yellow cord has a female twist-lock end with recessed slots that plugs onto those male prongs.' Never show a female inlet box or a male cord end.
connector_gender_CRITICAL.real_world_analogy: Think of it like a standard wall outlet (female, recessed) vs. a plug (male, prongs out). The inlet box IS the outlet — it has prongs. The cord IS the plug — it has holes.

general_physics: Cords have TWO ends — trace the path from source to destination
Electricity flows in a circuit — everything must be connected to a source
During a power outage, wall outlets are DEAD unless fed by the generator through the panel
Extension cords sag under gravity — they drape over windowsills, across floors
Generator exhaust is visible — slight heat shimmer or fumes from the exhaust port
Cord gauge matters visually — generator cords are THICK (10-gauge), not thin lamp cords

comparison_split.left_treatment: Red header, red-tinted photo, red bullet box with X marks
comparison_split.right_treatment: Navy header, clean photo (real if possible), navy bullet box with gold checkmarks
comparison_split.bottom: Gold flat banner with CTA text in white Outfit bold

value_stack.layout: Navy gradient card. Gold accent line at top. Stacked items with gold checkmarks. Total at bottom in large gold Outfit 800.
value_stack.elements: Each item: gold checkmark icon + white Inter text + gold value amount right-aligned

testimonial.layout: Navy card or white card with navy border. Star rating in gold. Quote in italic Inter. Name and location below.

prompt_vocabulary._note: Use these exact phrases in Nano Banana prompts to get the right visual style.
style_descriptors: flat design, no 3D effects, no metallic textures
sharp text, high contrast, no watermarks

landing_page_ui_design_language._note: ALWAYS read this section before generating any ad. These are the exact visual patterns from the live landing page at backuppowerpro.com. Ads should feel like a natural extension of the page — same design vocabulary, same visual hierarchy, same color application.

hero_section.background: Full-bleed dark photo (house exterior at night, stormy sky). Dark overlay on photo creates contrast for white text.
hero_section.headline_style: Very large bold Outfit font. White for most words. Gold (#ffba00) used ONLY on the punchline — the last impactful line or phrase. Example: 'You Already Own the Generator. Now Make It Worth' (white) + 'Every Dollar.' (gold).
hero_section.body: White text, clean Inter, below headline. Short sentences.
hero_section.price_callout: Gold bold '$1,197–$1,497' inline with white supporting text (all-in · Licensed SC Electrician · Permit & inspection included)
hero_section.cta_button: LARGE full-width pill button, flat gold (#ffba00) background, bold navy text. 'Get Your Exact Price →'
hero_section.trust_badge: Frosted/dark pill with Google G logo + gold stars + '4.9 · 100+ Google reviews · Key Electric'
hero_section.sub_trust: Small white text below CTA: 'We text you — no salesperson visit, no BS · Greenville, SC'

section_overlines.style: Small uppercase text, wide letter-spacing (0.15em), flat gold (#ffba00), placed ABOVE the main section headline
section_overlines.usage: Every major section has a gold overline label above a large navy headline. This is one of the most distinctive design patterns on the page — use it in ad graphic layouts.

before_after_format.description: Two full-bleed stacked photos. Top = BEFORE (dark, moody, orange cord through window). Bottom = AFTER (bright, clean, yellow cord plugged into Reliance inlet).
before_after_format.before_pill: Red pill label 'BEFORE' — rounded rectangle, red background, white text, positioned bottom-left of photo
before_after_format.after_pill: Green pill label 'AFTER' — rounded rectangle, green background, white text, positioned bottom-left of photo
before_after_format.captions: White text captions at bottom-left of each photo. Before: '1 appliance. 1 cord. Window cracked open in a storm.' After: 'All your essentials. One cord. Every window closed.'
before_after_format.use_in_ads: This before/after format with pill labels is highly recognizable from the site — replicate in comparison ads for message match.

lifestyle_photo_style.description: Upscale kitchen with couple — man standing, woman seated, warm interior light, white cabinetry, marble countertop, bowl of fruit, coffee maker. High-end home, $500k+ aesthetic.
lifestyle_photo_style.caption: White text overlay at bottom: 'Home investment becomes a family investment.'
lifestyle_photo_style.message: Shows that this service is for families who care about their home investment — not just 'prepper' types. Aspirational, warm, upper-middle-class.
lifestyle_photo_style.use_in_ads: Lifestyle/family angle ads should mirror this: upscale kitchen or living room interior, warm natural light, couple or family in background, home is clearly well-maintained and valued.

section_backgrounds.secondary: Light blue (#e8f1f8) — alternating sections, reviews area, form area
section_backgrounds.dark: Deep navy gradient (#0b1f3b → #1a3a5c) — pricing section, footer CTA
section_backgrounds.cream: Light yellow-cream — used for the pricing comparison card (standby vs BPP)

pricing_comparison_card.background: Light cream/yellow card on navy section background
pricing_comparison_card.competitor_price: Red (#dc2626) strikethrough on '$15,000–$20,000'. Supporting gray text: 'Plus concrete pad. Plus gas line. 2-3 month lead time. Annual maintenance.'
pricing_comparison_card.bpp_price: Large bold navy '$1,197' with smaller '–$1,497' beside it. Supporting text: 'All-in. Permit, cord, inspection, everything. One day.'
pricing_comparison_card.use_in_ads: The red strikethrough on competitor price vs clean navy BPP price is very effective visual pattern — replicate in value comparison ads.

checklist_items.style: Each item: small checkmark icon (navy circle with white check) + bold navy text + gold 'Included' right-aligned
checklist_items.background: White rounded-corner cards with subtle border on light blue section background
examples: Complete install by licensed SC electrician
checklist_items.use_in_ads: This checklist visual pattern works well in 'everything included' style ads.

cta_button_styles.primary_gold: Large pill button, flat gold (#ffba00) background, bold navy text 'Get Your Exact Price →'. Full-width on mobile. Used throughout page.
cta_button_styles.sticky_navy: Smaller dark navy pill button fixed at page bottom: 'Get Your Exact Price →' in white.
cta_button_styles.form_button: Full-width gold pill 'Text Me My Price →'. Below it: small gray text 'We'll text you from (864) 863-7800. No spam, no sales calls.'
cta_button_styles.note: Gold is the primary action color. Navy is the secondary. NEVER use any other color for CTAs.

typography_in_context.display_headline: Outfit 800, very large (48-72px equivalent), dark navy #0b1f3b, tight tracking. Sometimes one line white + one line gold.
typography_in_context.section_headline: Outfit 700, large (32-40px), dark navy, tight tracking.
typography_in_context.bold_callout: Outfit 700, mid-size, used for key statements like 'Your essentials stay on. No $15,000 standby required.'
typography_in_context.price_display: Outfit 800, very large, gold or navy depending on context.

photo_treatment_on_page.style: Full-bleed rounded-corner photos (border-radius ~16px). Real iPhone-quality. No filters or heavy editing. Natural light preferred.
photo_treatment_on_page.captions: White text overlaid at bottom of photo on semi-transparent dark gradient bar, or directly on photo if contrast allows.
photo_treatment_on_page.gallery: Stacked full-width photos (not grid). Each photo fills the full content width.

winning_ad_patterns._note: These are BPP's 2 currently active ads observed directly in the Meta Ad Library on March 24, 2026. Study these patterns — they're getting budget and impressions. New ads should build on what's already working.

ad_1_video.creative: Key speaking directly to camera outdoors. Close-up face shot. Natural background (outside, warm light). Bold captions overlaid: 'stop running extension' in green and white bold text on dark background.
ad_1_video.body_copy: Every storm season, the same thing — dig out the generator, string extension cords everywhere, pray nothing trips. There's a permanent fix. One-day inlet and interlock installation. Your home's essentials run off that same generator, through the panel. Done right.
ad_1_video.headline: There's a Safer Way to Use Your Generator
ad_1_video.description: One-day inlet and interlock installation. Permit and inspection included. Free quote. Serving Greenville County, SC.
key_insights: Key's face is the thumbnail — real person = highest trust signal
Bold caption text over video is essential — most people watch without sound
Pain-first copy: starts with the frustrating routine before offering the fix
Direct to camera, outdoor setting, natural and unstaged
9 versions = Meta is testing this heavily — the format is working

ad_2_static_illustration.creative: White background. Navy BPP logo at top center. Below: horizontal line of brand line-art illustrations — portable generator → inlet box → breaker panel (using the Untitled_Artwork brand illustrations). Clean, minimal.
ad_2_static_illustration.body_copy: Your portable generator can do way more than run a fridge and a lamp. A generator inlet and interlock connects it to your breaker panel — one cord, your home's essentials running safely. No extension cords. Licensed SC electrician. Installed in one day. Get your exact price — free 2-min quote.
ad_2_static_illustration.headline: Generator + Inlet + Interlock = Done Right
ad_2_static_illustration.description: Your generator + two parts = your home's essentials covered. Starting at $1,197. Licensed SC electrician.
key_insights: EQUATION FORMAT headline works: 'X + Y + Z = Result' — simple, scannable, clear
Brand line-art illustrations (NOT photos) are getting budget — the simple illustration style works
White background with navy = clean, professional, no visual noise
Copy explains the 'what' simply without jargon
Price anchor ($1,197) in description = good for qualifying leads

patterns_to_replicate: Equation-format headlines: 'X + Y = Z' or 'One cord. One connection. Your essentials stay on.'
Line-art brand illustrations on white/clean backgrounds — proven format that's getting spend
Pain-first body copy: describe the frustrating problem first, then position the fix
Key's face as the main creative element — real person outperforms everything else
Bold caption-style text overlays (even on static images) — high contrast, readable at thumb size
Gold CTA buttons matching the landing page for visual continuity
Section overline pattern from landing page: small gold uppercase label above headline
Before/after pill labels (red/green) mirroring the landing page's before/after section

landing_page_copy_bank._note: ALWAYS pull from this copy bank when writing ad text. This is the actual voice from the landing page. Using this language creates message match between the ad and the landing page, which improves lead quality and conversion rates.
headlines: Stop Running Extension Cords Through Your Window.
One connection to your breaker panel. Your essentials stay on. Done in one day.
subheads_and_body: Right now, it's limited to one cord, one circuit, one window cracked open.
Without a panel connection, you're running one cord to one thing while the food spoils, the sump pump stops, and the family sits in the dark.
You already own the generator — we unlock your existing investment.
Your essentials stay on. No $15,000 standby required.
A fraction of the $15,000+ standby generator alternative.
Completed in a single day. Permitted, inspected, and fully operational before we leave.
All-in. Permit, cord, inspection, everything. One day.
No hidden fees, no upsells, no 'well actually' on install day.
Home investment becomes a family investment.
We text you — no salesperson visit, no BS.
wrong_way_copy: 1 appliance. 1 cord. Window cracked open in a storm.
Limited to one or two things at a time.
You have to crack a window open — even in a storm.
Code violation that can void your homeowner's insurance.
The food spoils, the sump pump stops, and the family sits in the dark.
right_way_copy: All your essentials. One cord. Every window closed.
Power your essentials — fridge, lights, WiFi, well pump, sump pump, and more — all at once.
No extension cords, no open windows.
Permitted, inspected, and fully operational before we leave.
Licensed, insured, permitted, and inspected on every single job.
process_copy: Fill out the 30-second form. We text you your all-in price the same day.
Once you approve, we handle the permit.
On install day, we connect your generator directly to your panel.
Takes 30 seconds. We'll text you your all-in number today.
We'll text you from (864) 863-7800. No spam, no sales calls.
trust_signals: The price we quote is the price you pay. Period.
If anything we installed fails within 1 year, we fix it free.
5 install slots per week · Greenville, SC
pricing_copy: All-in — that's the cost to connect your existing generator to your panel.
Covers the inlet hardware, permit, county inspection, generator cord, and a full walkthrough.
A whole-home standby generator starts at $15,000 installed.
We connect what you already own for a fraction of that.

real_customer_reviews.text: Key moved/wired several breakers, drilled through a brick wall, mounted a generator outlet and installed an interlock. He was very responsive, excellent communication and was very reasonable about an unexpected issue. The work was very professional and no mess left behind. Highly recommended.
Key recently installed a 50 amp generator plug and interlock system in our home and did an amazing job! He worked around my schedule and he was punctual, meticulous and professional.
Key was courteous and on time. He installed a generator inlet for me and the work looks good and he cleaned up well. Key is a good communicator that runs a very well run little business.
real_customer_reviews.name: Christopher Capps

real_customer_reviews.text: Key was awesome!! He's super nice, organized and CLEAN!! He kept his tools organized and wore shoe covers in the house. He came and added a 50 amp generator plug and interlock kit at my house. I would recommend Key to anyone.

landing_page_copy_bank.secondary_cta: Takes 30 seconds. We'll text you your all-in number today.

premium_positioning._note: BPP is a premium category of one. We do NOT describe ourselves using commodity hardware names or generic trade language. We use aspirational, outcome-focused category language that positions this as a premium home improvement — not a handyman job.
NEVER_use_commodity_language: Do NOT say 'Reliance Controls inlet' in ads — say 'outage-ready home connection point' or 'whole-home connection'
Do NOT say 'interlock kit' in ads — say 'whole-panel safety interlock' or 'panel protection system'
Do NOT say 'we install generator inlets' — say 'we create outage-ready homes'
Do NOT say 'generator inlet box' — say 'home connection point' or 'whole-home connection port'
Do NOT describe the service as a 'hookup' or 'plug-in' — position it as a permanent home improvement

instead_of_install_generator_inlet: connect your generator to your entire panel
make your home outage-ready in one day
instead_of_extension_cords: the dangerous workaround most people use

positioning_statements: A permanent upgrade — not a workaround.
The last thing standing between your family and a powerless home.
Outage-ready isn't a product. It's a decision.
One connection. Every essential. One day.
Your generator was always capable of this. We just make it official.
This is what separates the house that stays on from every other house on the block.
premium_positioning.category_of_one_strategy: We are not 'an electrician who does generator hookups.' We are the Upstate SC specialists in Outage-Ready Home Connections. No competitor owns this language. Use it consistently across all ads, copy, and content to create a proprietary category that only we fill.

reference_photos._note: ALWAYS inject these real reference photos into any Gemini prompt involving the inlet box or Key. Text prompts alone will NEVER produce accurate hardware or a correct likeness. Inject as base64 inlineData — see creative_director_workflow for how.

inlet_box.what_they_show: Gray rectangular cast-metal Reliance Controls inlet box. Round hinged dome cover with swing-arm latch. 'RELIANCE' embossed on cover. When closed: round disc-shaped cover latched shut. When open: circular L14-30R receptacle exposed, cover swung down. Yellow L14-30 cord plugs straight into the round receptacle. Gray conduit exits bottom. Box is ~6 inches tall x 5 inches wide. Mounts on brick, siding, or block.

key_goodson.what_they_show: White male, early-to-mid 20s, round face, short brown/dirty-blonde hair (short and neat), light stubble or clean-shaven, friendly smile, solid/broad build. Often wears light blue KEY ELECTRIC work shirt. Stands in front of KEY ELECTRIC work van (tan/white with blue lettering).

creative_director_workflow._note: MANDATORY PROCESS for every BPP photo generation. DO NOT skip steps. This process exists because quick prompting produces 40/100 results. The brief-first workflow produces 80/100+ results.

STEP_1_BRIEF.rule: Before writing a single prompt, define: (1) What does a cold stranger understand about this image in 1.5 seconds? (2) What is the ONE emotion it creates? (3) What specific visual element carries the message? If you cannot answer all three, do not proceed.
STEP_1_BRIEF.scroll_test: Imagine someone scrolling Facebook at 60mph. If they need context to understand what they're seeing, the concept fails. Redesign until it's instantly self-explanatory.

STEP_2_REFERENCE_INJECTION.rule: For ANY image showing the inlet box OR Key: INJECT REFERENCE PHOTOS. This is non-negotiable. Text descriptions will produce wrong hardware and wrong face every time. Reference injection is the only path to accuracy.
STEP_2_REFERENCE_INJECTION.how: Load reference images as base64 and pass them as inlineData parts BEFORE the text prompt in the Gemini API call contents array. The model uses them as visual anchors.

STEP_3_DRAFT_AT_1K.rule: Always generate at 1K first (imageSize: '1K') before going to 4K. 4K costs 4x more API time, burns 503 quota, and if the composition fails you wasted the call. Check the draft first.
STEP_3_DRAFT_AT_1K.exception: Only skip to 4K if the exact same prompt has already produced a verified 1K pass

STEP_4_EVALUATE_CHECKLIST.rule: Score the 1K draft on ALL 5 checks before promoting to 4K. Fail on any = revise and re-draft.
checks: 1. SELF-EXPLANATORY: Does a cold stranger immediately understand what they're seeing without context?
2. HARDWARE ACCURACY: Does the inlet box look exactly like the reference photos? Gray cast-metal, round dome cover, correct scale (~6 inches), no doubled elements?
3. CORD PHYSICS: Does the cord have exactly two endpoints? Does it drape naturally under gravity? Does it go the right direction? No loops, no crossings, no floats?
4. PERSON ACCURACY: If Key is in the image, does the face match the reference? Young white male, short brown hair, broad build?
5. UNIFORMITY: Are siding boards identical width? Brick courses level? No doubled elements?

STEP_5_PROMOTE_TO_4K.rule: Use the IDENTICAL prompt that passed Step 4. Do NOT rewrite for 4K — just change imageSize to '4K'. Save to march-2026-campaign/ folder.
STEP_5_PROMOTE_TO_4K.on_503_error: Retry the same script. Do not change the prompt between retries.

ai_photo_generation_critical_rules._note: MUST READ BEFORE GENERATING ANY PHOTO. These rules address the most common AI generation failures observed in our ads. Every single rule is non-negotiable.

NO_TEXT_IN_PHOTOS.rule: NEVER embed text, labels, counters, copy, overlays, or design elements into AI-generated photos. Generate CLEAN PHOTOS ONLY. All ad copy, headlines, CTAs, frame counters, gold bars, and brand elements get added separately in Meta Ads Manager or design tools. Baking text into photos creates font rendering errors, garbled text, and design artifacts.
NO_TEXT_IN_PHOTOS.exception: AD02 price card and similar pure graphic design ads are intentional text-heavy graphics — text is expected in those. For any image that is primarily photographic (person, house, install shot, scene), generate with ZERO text.

NO_DOUBLED_ELEMENTS.rule: AI models frequently generate two versions of the same element in one frame (two inlet covers, two generators, two cord connectors, two covers in different positions). Prevent this explicitly in every prompt.
prompt_phrases_to_include: exactly one generator in the scene
exactly one inlet box on the wall
exactly one inlet cover — either fully open OR fully closed, not both
a single yellow cord runs from the generator to the inlet, no other cords visible
no duplicate objects, no mirrored elements

CORD_PHYSICS_RULES.rule: CORD GENERATION IS THE #1 AI FAILURE POINT. Every single prompt involving a cord MUST include hyper-specific language describing both endpoints, the full path, and the direction. Without this, the model will draw a cord that floats, connects to nothing, goes the wrong direction, or passes through a closed surface. These rules are MANDATORY — do not generate any cord scene without them.
CORD_PHYSICS_RULES.MANDATORY_CORD_LANGUAGE_BLOCK: Copy and paste this exact block into EVERY cord prompt, customized for the specific scene: 'The [COLOR] cord has TWO specific endpoints. ENDPOINT A: [exact start — e.g., the outlet port on the LEFT side of the generator]. ENDPOINT B: [exact end — e.g., the inlet box socket on the house wall]. The cord runs FROM Endpoint A TO Endpoint B in a continuous, unbroken path with no floating ends, no breaks, and no sections that disappear. The cord drapes naturally under gravity — it does not float or hover. The complete cord path is visible in the frame from one end to the other.'

WINDOW_SCENE_CORD_LANGUAGE.rule: For the 'wrong way' scene with cord through a window, use ALL of these exact phrases:
required_phrases: The lower sash of the window is physically raised UP [X] inches from the bottom, creating a visible horizontal gap between the bottom of the lower sash and the window sill
The window is NOT closed — there is a clear open gap at the bottom where the lower sash has been pushed up
The orange extension cord physically passes THROUGH this open gap — entering from the dark exterior outside, crossing over the window sill, and continuing inside the room
You can see the cord on BOTH sides of the window: outside (in darkness) and inside (on the floor)
The cord does NOT pass through glass — it passes through the open gap where the window is raised
The cord drapes over the window sill from outside to inside in a single continuous path from [outside origin] through the gap to [inside destination like power strip]

GENERATOR_TO_INLET_CORD_LANGUAGE.rule: For generator-to-inlet scenes, use ALL of these exact phrases:
required_phrases: The yellow L14-30 cord starts at the outlet port on the [LEFT/RIGHT/FRONT] side of the generator
The cord runs FROM the generator TOWARD the house wall — not away from the house, not to the side, but directly toward the inlet box on the wall
The cord crosses the concrete driveway in a natural gravity drape from the generator to the inlet box
The cord TERMINATES at the inlet box on the house wall — you can see both the generator end and the inlet box end
The cord path goes: generator outlet → across driveway → inlet box on wall. This is the complete path with no other destinations.
No part of the cord goes away from the house or into the yard or off-frame without connecting to something

PERSON_PLUGGING_IN_CORD_LANGUAGE.rule: For scenes with a person plugging in a cord, use ALL of these phrases:
required_phrases: The person holds the cord connector in their hands at the inlet box
The cord runs from the person's hands across the driveway in a single continuous drape to the generator in the background
Both ends of the cord are visible: the connector in the person's hands (near end) and the generator outlet (far end)
The cord does not loop back on itself, does not go off-frame without a destination, and does not float

SIDING_UNIFORMITY_CRITICAL.rule: Every lap siding board must be IDENTICAL in width. This is one of the clearest AI tells — models often vary board widths randomly. Vinyl and fiber cement lap siding has factory-uniform board exposure (the visible face of each board). All boards must be exactly the same height/width from top to bottom of the wall.
prompt_phrases_to_include: all siding boards are identical in width and exposure
perfectly uniform lap siding with every board the same height
no variation in siding board width — every course is equal

INLET_SIZE_RULE.rule: The Reliance Controls inlet box is SMALL — approximately 6 to 8 inches tall by 5 inches wide. It is roughly the size of a large hardcover book or a thick paperback. Do NOT let the model generate it at the size of a junction box, a breaker panel, or anything larger than a human hand. It should look small and proportionally correct on the wall.
prompt_phrases_to_include: the inlet box is small — about 6 inches tall, roughly the size of a hardcover book
the person's hands are larger than the inlet box
the inlet box is a compact wall-mounted unit, not a large enclosure

INLET_COVER_MECHANICS_VERIFIED.rule: The Reliance Controls inlet cover mechanics were verified through multiple generation attempts and a real reference photo. USE THIS EXACT DESCRIPTION every time the cord is plugged in and the cover must be shown open.

verified_cover_position_when_cord_plugge.hinge_location: TOP of the circular receptacle opening — a small metal hinge arm at the 12 o'clock position connects the cover to the box
verified_cover_position_when_cord_plugge.natural_hang_direction: Cover hangs DOWN from the top hinge by gravity (like a trap door hinged at the top)
verified_cover_position_when_cord_plugge.actual_position_with_cord: When the yellow cord is plugged in FROM THE LEFT, the cord physically pushes and holds the cover to the LEFT — cover ends up at 9-10 o'clock position (left side of the receptacle, roughly at center height)
verified_cover_position_when_cord_plugge.cover_face: Round gray disc with 'RELIANCE' embossed on its face, visible at an angle when open
verified_cover_position_when_cord_plugge.what_makes_it_look_real: The hinge arm is visibly connecting the cover to the box at 12 o'clock. The cover is NOT floating — it is clearly attached. The cord holds it to the left side.

INLET_COVER_MECHANICS_VERIFIED.MANDATORY_PROMPT_LANGUAGE_FOR_CORD_PLUGG: The round gray cover is hinged at the very TOP of the circular receptacle opening — a small metal hinge arm is visible at the 12 o'clock position. When the cord is plugged in from the LEFT, the cord physically pushes the cover to the LEFT so it rests at the 9-10 o'clock position (to the left of the receptacle, roughly at center height). The hinge arm clearly connects the cover to the box at the top — it is not a floating disc. The 'RELIANCE' text is visible at an angle on the cover face.
DO_NOT_describe_cover_as: hanging at the bottom (6 o'clock) — this looks wrong even if technically possible without cord pressure
floating or detached from the box

PERSON_DESCRIPTION_RULE.rule: Key Goodson (the business owner, the electrician in all person-based ads) is a white male, early-to-mid 20s, short brown hair, athletic/solid build, clean-cut with light stubble or clean-shaven. ALWAYS describe him accurately before using reference photos. Reference photos reinforce — text description anchors. If the text says the wrong race/age/appearance, the reference photos will NOT override it.
PERSON_DESCRIPTION_RULE.correct_description: white male, early-to-mid 20s, short brown hair (slightly textured on top), solid athletic build, broad shoulders, clean-cut
PERSON_DESCRIPTION_RULE._reference_note: Only one Key headshot is on disk (assets/images/key-headshot.jpg). Reference injection works best with 2-3 angles; add more Key photos before any high-stakes person-based generation.
